zones.name: Tests
zones.description: Non-source files in Tests: InterServiceWiringTests.swift, OverlayControllerTests.swift, SchedulerEngineTests.swift (+1 more)
insights: High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
4 circular dependency chains detected — see imports.json for details
Hub: Sources/GoToBedCore/Models/Schedule.swift is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency
Hub: Sources/GoToBedCore/Models/Validation.swift is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency
Hub: Sources/GoToBedCore/Store/Store.swift is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency
Bidirectional coupling: "app-core" ↔ "app-orchestration" (8+7 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
Size imbalance: largest zone has 24 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition
4 circular dependency chains detected — see imports.json for details
Bidirectional imports (8 from app-core to app-orchestration, 7 reverse) establish a clear dependency direction: orchestration consumes core models
Test coverage across both zones (27 imports to app-core, 4 to app-orchestration) confirms both are primary entry points

The app-orchestration zone's low cohesion reflects poor zone detection rather than architectural debt; OverlayController, SchedulerEngine, and Log would better live within their respective subsystem zones
File "Tests/GoToBedCoreTests/SubmessageTests.swift" has 100% of import neighbors in Sources/GoToBedCore/Models/ but lives in Tests/GoToBedCoreTests/ — consider relocating

The four circular dependency chains all originate in the settings UI subtree: three chains involve AppEnvironment ↔ SettingsWindowController ↔ SettingsView ↔ ScheduleEditorView (each a progressively longer cycle), and one cross-layer chain involves AppearanceSettings ↔ ColorBridging — the settings-related cycles are the primary structural risk area

SwiftUI @EnvironmentObject injection of AppEnvironment (the full composition root) into SettingsView and ScheduleEditorView means views depend on the entire app graph rather than a narrow action interface, preventing standalone Previews and inflating the dependency surface for every settings screen

The closure injection seam between SchedulerEngine and OverlayController (wired in AppEnvironment) is the sole mechanism preventing a direct orchestration-layer circular import — it is architecturally load-bearing but not recorded in any comment, protocol, or injection-seam registry

All four detected circular dependency chains converge on AppEnvironment as a shared node — it is simultaneously a composition root, a circular-dependency break point, and a SwiftUI environment provider, concentrating architectural risk in a single file with no documented invariants

The cross-layer cycle between AppearanceSettings (domain model in GoToBedCore) and ColorBridging (app-layer SwiftUI adapter in GoToBed) violates the expected dependency direction: domain models should have no knowledge of or extension by app-layer types; breaking this cycle requires either moving ColorBridging extensions into GoToBedCore or replacing the extension with a standalone mapping function in the app layer
